proposed_hypotheses.text: The binary outcome `objective_response` has a non-trivial overall rate (between 10% and 50%) so several features may be informative.
`feature_030` is constant in the cohort (all zeros) and therefore carries no information about `objective_response`.

analyses.code: df.objective_response.mean(); df.shape
analyses.result_summary: n=50000 rows; objective_response mean = 0.2402 (12,011 of 50,000 patients responded). Rate is well-suited to logistic-regression-style modelling.

analyses.code: df.feature_030.value_counts()
analyses.result_summary: feature_030 takes the value 0 for all 50,000 patients (zero variance); excluded from later analyses.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features, at least one shows a strong positive association with `objective_response` (absolute response-rate difference >= 0.10) consistent with a treatment assignment.
Several binary features show NEGATIVE associations with `objective_response` (response-rate difference <= -0.05), consistent with markers of high-risk disease.

analyses.code: for c in binary_features: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df[c], y))
analyses.result_summary: feature_008 has the strongest positive marginal association: response = 0.361 when feature_008==1 vs 0.159 when feature_008==0 (absolute diff +0.202, chi-square p < 1e-300).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency for feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027
analyses.result_summary: Four binary features show large negative marginal associations: feature_013 (+1 -> response 0.154 vs 0.346, diff -0.193, p < 1e-300), feature_015 (-0.100, p=1.9e-98), feature_021 (-0.100, p=5.4e-56), and feature_027 (-0.066, p=3.0e-9). All are highly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: If `feature_008` is a randomized treatment assignment then it should be balanced (no statistically meaningful differences) on the other 32 features.

analyses.code: for c in features: compare mean(c | feature_008==1) vs mean(c | feature_008==0)
analyses.result_summary: Across all 32 other features the absolute difference between feature_008 arms is < 0.006 in proportion / < 0.4 in median for every variable (e.g., feature_013: 0.5498 vs 0.5495; feature_022 PSA-like: median 15.82 vs 15.89; age-like feature_016 medians 64.9 vs 65.0). Balance is so tight that feature_008 is effectively a randomized treatment indicator.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment with `feature_008==1` increases the probability of `objective_response` relative to `feature_008==0`, with an unadjusted odds ratio >= 2.5.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ feature_008)
analyses.result_summary: Univariable logistic regression: log-OR = 1.10, OR = 3.00 (95% CI ~2.87-3.14), p < 1e-300. Treatment-arm response rate 36.1% vs control 15.9%. Marginal absolute benefit +20.2 percentage points.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_022` (continuous, range 0.12-3622, median 15.86) behaves like a serum PSA value and is inversely associated with `objective_response`.
`feature_010` (integer 6-10, mode 7-8) behaves like Gleason sum and is NOT a strong predictor of `objective_response` after baseline adjustment.
`feature_001` (ordinal 0/1/2) is a prognostic factor (likely performance status), with response rate strictly decreasing across levels.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ log(feature_022))
analyses.result_summary: log(feature_022) effect on response: coef = -0.215 (Welch t-test diff in PSA between responders/non-responders = -19.0, p = 1.5e-137). Higher values predict lower response, consistent with PSA.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ feature_010) and adjusted
analyses.result_summary: feature_010 marginal chi-square p = 0.77; in the full multivariable model coef = +0.0004 (p = 0.97). Not a meaningful predictor of response in either crude or adjusted analysis.

analyses.code: y.groupby(df.feature_001).mean()
analyses.result_summary: Response rate by feature_001 level: 0=0.282 (n=17,592), 1=0.228 (n=24,971), 2=0.182 (n=7,437); strictly monotonically decreasing. Multivariable logistic coef = -0.328 per level (p = 5.6e-72).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of `objective_response` on all features, `feature_008` retains the largest positive coefficient and `feature_013`, `feature_015`, `feature_021`, `feature_027` retain large negative coefficients, indicating their associations are not confounded.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ all features + log-transforms of skewed labs)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-ORs (Wald p): feature_008 +1.20 (p<1e-300); feature_013 -1.07 (p<1e-300); feature_021 -0.74 (p=4e-64); feature_015 -0.69 (p=2e-108); feature_027 -0.43 (p=3e-9); feature_001 -0.33 (p=2e-82); feature_002 +0.13 (p=2e-8); log(feature_022) -0.07 (p=6e-12); feature_020 -0.04 (p=1e-33). Pseudo-R^2 = 0.125. Strong predictors are not confounded by other measured variables.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_022` (PSA-like) is markedly higher in patients with `feature_013==1` (median ~33) than in `feature_013==0` (median ~7), suggesting feature_013 is a marker of more advanced disease stage rather than a randomized arm.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_013').feature_022.median()
analyses.result_summary: Median feature_022 = 7.38 if feature_013==0 vs 32.94 if feature_013==1 (Mann-Whitney p < 1e-300). All other features are balanced across feature_013, so feature_013 marks a disease state correlated with PSA, not an independent randomization.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of `feature_008` on `objective_response` is heterogeneous across the cohort: at least one feature significantly modifies the treatment-by-outcome relationship.

analyses.code: for each feature c: Logit(y ~ feature_008 + c + feature_008*c)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction screen across all 31 candidate features identified 4 features with extremely strong negative interaction with feature_008: feature_013 (interaction OR 0.138, p<1e-300), feature_015 (OR 0.290, p=1.4e-93), feature_021 (OR 0.321, p=3.3e-42), feature_027 (OR 0.277, p=1.8e-19). log(feature_022) interaction OR 0.447 (p=1.2e-72). All other interaction p-values >> 0.001 except weak ones for feature_001 (p=1.6e-5, OR 1.15) and feature_011 (p=0.02, OR 0.90).

proposed_hypotheses.text: When `feature_013==0`, treatment with `feature_008==1` substantially raises `objective_response` (absolute increase >= 0.30); when `feature_013==1`, the treatment effect is essentially zero.

analyses.code: stratified rates: y ~ feature_008 by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: feature_013==0 (n=22,519): treatment 0.539 vs control 0.099, absolute diff +0.440, chi-square p<1e-300. feature_013==1 (n=27,481): treatment 0.218 vs control 0.211, absolute diff +0.008, p=0.13. Treatment essentially inert in the feature_013==1 stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When `feature_015==1`, the treatment effect of `feature_008` on `objective_response` is essentially zero, mirroring the feature_013 pattern.
When `feature_021==1`, the treatment effect of `feature_008` on `objective_response` is essentially zero.
When `feature_027==1` (rare ~3% subgroup), the treatment effect of `feature_008` on `objective_response` is essentially zero or even slightly negative.

analyses.code: stratified rates by feature_015
analyses.result_summary: feature_015==0 (n=39,962): trt 0.435 vs ctrl 0.184, abs diff +0.251, p<1e-300. feature_015==1 (n=10,038): trt 0.067 vs ctrl 0.058, abs diff +0.009, p=0.21. Confirms feature_015==1 nullifies the treatment effect.

analyses.code: stratified rates by feature_021
analyses.result_summary: feature_021==0 (n=45,004): trt 0.385 vs ctrl 0.161, abs diff +0.225, p<1e-300. feature_021==1 (n=4,996): trt 0.040 vs ctrl 0.034, abs diff +0.006, p=0.46. Confirms feature_021==1 nullifies the treatment effect.

analyses.code: stratified rates by feature_027
analyses.result_summary: feature_027==0 (n=48,472): trt 0.367 vs ctrl 0.158, abs diff +0.209, p<1e-300. feature_027==1 (n=1,528): trt 0.064 vs ctrl 0.087, abs diff -0.022, p=0.21. Confirms feature_027==1 abolishes treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The four resistance features (`feature_013`, `feature_015`, `feature_021`, `feature_027`) act in approximately Boolean-OR fashion: presence of ANY of them is sufficient to abolish the treatment effect of `feature_008`, while patients with NONE of them obtain a very large treatment benefit.

analyses.code: stratify by 16 combinations of (feature_013,015,021,027); compute treatment effect within each
analyses.result_summary: Combination 0000 (n=15,681): trt 0.798 vs ctrl 0.172, diff +0.626. All other 11 combinations with n>=100 show treatment effects between -0.084 and +0.088 (median |effect| 0.022). Boolean-OR pattern is confirmed: any flag set drops the treatment effect to near zero; only the all-zeros stratum responds.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four resistance flags (`feature_013`, `feature_015`, `feature_021`, `feature_027`) is INDIVIDUALLY necessary in the subgroup definition: holding the other three at 0, switching one flag to 1 collapses the treatment effect from ~+0.63 to near zero.

analyses.code: necessity test: for each modifier m, compare trt effect within (other_3==0) by m=0 vs m=1
analyses.result_summary: feature_013: 0->+0.626 vs 1->+0.007 (n=19,163); feature_015: 0->+0.626 vs 1->+0.022 (n=3,940); feature_021: 0->+0.626 vs 1->+0.004 (n=1,775); feature_027: 0->+0.626 vs 1->-0.025 (n=494). Each of the four flags is individually necessary; none can be dropped without losing the predictive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After defining `good_subgroup = (feature_013==0 AND feature_015==0 AND feature_021==0 AND feature_027==0)`, the treatment*good_subgroup interaction in a logistic model is highly significant and dwarfs the main effect of treatment.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ trt + good + trt:good)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +2.885 (z=56.1, p<1e-300); main treatment coef in NOT-good = +0.058 (p=0.055, OR 1.06); treatment OR within good_subgroup = exp(0.058+2.885) = 18.98. Pseudo-R^2 jumps from 0.027 (treatment alone) to 0.190 with the interaction term, the largest single explanatory jump in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the good subgroup (n=15,681), `feature_022` (PSA-like) does NOT meaningfully attenuate the treatment effect across PSA quartiles.

analyses.code: split good subgroup by PSA quartile, compute trt-control diff
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect by PSA quartile within good subgroup: Q1 (PSA 0.14-3.8) +0.633; Q2 (3.8-7.4) +0.628; Q3 (7.4-14.4) +0.624; Q4 (14.4-356.8) +0.618. Effect is essentially constant across the PSA range (range 0.015). PSA does not refine the predictive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the good subgroup, NO additional measured continuous or categorical feature meaningfully modifies the treatment effect of `feature_008` (after Bonferroni correction across ~26 candidate features).

analyses.code: for each remaining feature c: Logit(y ~ trt + c + trt:c) on good subgroup (n=15681)
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p-values within the good subgroup were feature_005 (p=0.004, int_OR 0.78), feature_017 (p=0.033, int_OR 0.75), feature_001 (p=0.069, int_OR 0.89). With 26 tests, Bonferroni threshold ~0.0019 -> none survive correction. The good_subgroup definition is essentially complete; no additional modifier is needed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the CONTROL arm only (feature_008==0), `feature_001`, `feature_002`, `log(feature_022)`, `feature_020`, and `log(feature_024)` are the strongest baseline prognostic factors for `objective_response`, while feature_013/015/021/027 have only weak independent prognostic value.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ all features) on control arm only (n=29,924)
analyses.result_summary: Control-arm coefficients: feature_001 -0.380 (p=6e-55); feature_002 +0.151 (p=2e-6); log(feature_022) -0.071 (p=7e-7); log(feature_024) -0.063 (p=2e-5); feature_020 -0.038 (p=4e-18). The four 'resistance' flags have small/non-significant coefficients in controls (013 -0.046 p=0.23; 015 -0.069 p=0.13; 021 -0.100 p=0.07; 027 +0.188 p=0.03). Their dominant role is therefore PREDICTIVE of treatment effect, not PROGNOSTIC of baseline outcome.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the TREATMENT arm only (feature_008==1), feature_013/015/021/027 dominate as predictors of response; their adjusted log-OR magnitudes exceed 1.4 each, far larger than any other feature.

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ all features) on treatment arm only (n=20,076)
analyses.result_summary: Treatment-arm coefficients: feature_013 -2.27 (p<1e-300); feature_015 -1.66 (p=5e-226); feature_021 -1.65 (p=2e-118); feature_027 -1.48 (p=3e-31); feature_001 -0.34 (p=3e-36); feature_002 +0.12 (p=4e-4); log(feature_022) -0.08 (p=1e-6); log(feature_024) -0.07 (p=2e-5); feature_020 -0.04 (p=1e-18). Pure prognostic factors have similar coefficients to those in controls; the four 'resistance' flags are an order of magnitude larger than in controls -> strong predictive signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the cohort, response rate is monotonically decreasing in the count of resistance flags set (0 to 4), but only when the patient is treated; in controls the rate is roughly flat across the count.

analyses.code: df['flag_count']=df[['feature_013','feature_015','feature_021','feature_027']].sum(axis=1); cross-tab
analyses.result_summary: Treated (feature_008==1) response by flag_count: 0->0.798; 1->0.169; 2->0.158; 3->~0.14; 4-> too few. Control (feature_008==0) response by flag_count: 0->0.172; 1->0.151; 2->~0.146; 3->~0.16. Trt benefit is concentrated almost entirely at flag_count=0; once any flag is set the response collapses to control-like levels and stays there.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The number-needed-to-treat (NNT) for `feature_008` to produce one additional `objective_response` is approximately 1.6 in the good subgroup and infinite (no benefit) in the complementary subgroup.

analyses.code: ARR and NNT in good subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: Good subgroup (n=15,681): ARR = 0.626, NNT = 1.60, RR = 4.64, OR = 18.98. Complement (n=34,319): ARR = 0.013, NNT ~76 (95% CI crosses infinity), OR = 1.06. Treatment is highly effective only in the good subgroup; outside it the drug has no clinically meaningful benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP: Among patients with `feature_013==0 AND feature_015==0 AND feature_021==0 AND feature_027==0` (~31% of the cohort), assignment to `feature_008==1` increases `objective_response` from 17.2% to 79.8% (absolute +62.6 pp, OR ~19); in the complementary 69% of patients, `feature_008==1` confers no detectable benefit (absolute +1.3 pp, OR 1.06).

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ trt + good + trt:good) and stratified rates
analyses.result_summary: Verified one final time. Subgroup size: 15,681/50,000 (31.4%). Within subgroup: 5,649 treated -> 4,506 responders (79.8%); 10,032 controls -> 1,727 responders (17.2%); chi-square p<1e-300; ARR +0.626; NNT 1.60. Outside subgroup: 14,427 treated -> 2,393 responders (16.6%); 19,892 controls -> 3,037 responders (15.3%); ARR +0.013; trt OR 1.06 (p=0.055). Interaction term in logistic model p<1e-300. This is the best-supported predictive subgroup for the feature_008 -> objective_response relationship and all four flags are individually necessary in its definition.
